summary: Matched two ftCommon source files, ftCo_PassiveWall.c and ftCo_0C35.c, removed their corresponding asm objects, and improved ftCo_AirCatch.c matching. The central struct lesson was that CollData was four bytes too large: ecb_lock was moved out of CollData into owning Fighter and Item structs, which made a CollData struct-copy pattern in ftCo_800C3A14 plausible and improved matching.

key_files.role: Converted from NonMatching to Matching with small expression, float literal, and stack padding adjustments.
Converted from NonMatching to Matching; removed forced literal/data definitions and adjusted inline/decrement ordering.
Improved score by replacing a hand-written copy loop with CollData coll = *getFtColl(fp), adding PAD_STACK, and simplifying control flow.
Removed ecb_lock from struct CollData, reducing its size by four bytes.
Added Fighter::ecb_lock immediately after Fighter::coll_data at fp+88C.
Added Item::ecb_lock after Item::x378_itemColl and an unknown field after ItemLink::x30_collData.
Updated ecb_lock uses from fp->coll_data.ecb_lock to fp->ecb_lock.
Updated common fighter collision/ground/air state helpers to use Fighter::ecb_lock.
Updated item owner/collision lock bookkeeping to use Item::ecb_lock instead of x378_itemColl.ecb_lock.
Changed ftCo_PassiveWall.c and ftCo_0C35.c objects from NonMatching to Matching.
Swapped ftCo_PassiveWall and ftCo_0C35 object paths from asm .s.o to src .c.o.

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveWall.s and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C35.s after matching their C sources.
Marked melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveWall.c and melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C35.c as Matching in configure.py.
Changed obj_files.mk to build ftCo_PassiveWall.c.o and ftCo_0C35.c.o from src instead of asm.
Removed ecb_lock from struct CollData in src/melee/lb/types.h.
Added ecb_lock as a field after coll_data in Fighter and after x378_itemColl in Item, then updated fighter and item call sites accordingly.
In ftCo_AirCatch.c, replaced an odd manual loop copying collision data with CollData coll = *getFtColl(fp), matching the PR note that the loop was a CollData struct copy.
In ftCo_0C35.c, removed #pragma force_active and local literal/data definitions for S32_TO_F32, zero, M_PI, M_PI_2, jobj strings, and the quaternion assert string.
In ftCo_0C35.c, changed static inline void inlineA0 to static void inlineA0 and moved the x18F4 decrement to occur after computing rot_y.
In ftCo_PassiveWall.c, used explicit float literals, PAD_STACK, and equivalent decimal constants to improve codegen.
In ftCo_AirCatch.c, introduced a local drift variable, explicit f64 comparison against 0.0F, PAD_STACK, and removed goto-based exit labels in favor of structured fallthrough.
smart_moves: Identified a decompiler-looking loop in ftCo_800C3A14 as a direct CollData struct copy instead of preserving the misleading manual IntVec3 loop.
Used the CollData copy mismatch to infer a struct boundary problem: ecb_lock should not be inside CollData, because CollData was four bytes too large.
Moved ecb_lock to the owner structs rather than adding padding or forcing the loop, preserving more accurate type layout for both fighters and items.
Updated all known fp->coll_data.ecb_lock and item collision lock uses consistently after moving the field.
Removed force_active literal declarations from matched files instead of keeping artificial data in C after the object was converted to Matching.
Used PAD_STACK where explicit dummy byte arrays were only serving stack-shape/matching needs.
Kept ftCo_AirCatch.c NonMatching but improved its score, avoiding overclaiming a full match where only an improvement was evidenced.
decomp_lessons: When a copied subobject emits as a strange loop, test whether the source type size is wrong before hand-matching the loop literally.
A four-byte overrun or missing tail field can prevent clean struct-copy codegen; moving a field out to the owning struct can be the right fix if offsets support it.
Do not assume a field belongs to an embedded struct just because its offset immediately follows that struct in one owner; confirm with copy behavior and other owners.
Removing a member from a shared struct requires updating every owner-specific access path, such as Fighter::ecb_lock and Item::ecb_lock.
For matching C, small expression-order changes matter: ftCo_0C35 computes rot_y before decrementing fp->dmg.x18F4, then decrements within the same block.
Use explicit float constants such as 0.0F and 1.0F when integer literals perturb codegen in float-argument calls.
PAD_STACK is preferable to meaningless initialized local arrays when the variable only exists to force stack layout.
Reviewer guidance suggests that if only a data section is failing to match, unlinking/reverting that object to NonMatching can be acceptable, but struct breakage should be fixed first.
naming_conventions: Retained existing unknown ftCommon symbol names such as ftCo_800C1D38, ftCo_800C1E0C, ftCo_800C3598, ftCo_800C36DC, ftCo_800C37A0, and ftCo_800C3A14 where no semantic names were evidenced.
Used ecb_lock as the field name after moving it out of CollData into Fighter and Item.
Preserved existing collision naming: Fighter::coll_data, Item::x378_itemColl, ItemLink::x30_collData, and getFtColl(fp).
The PR did not provide new semantic names for PassiveWall, 0C35 internals, or the ItemLink unknown field; it used UNK_T unk for the added ItemLink slot.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted matched asm files and redirected obj_files.mk entries from asm .s.o to src .c.o.
Changed configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching for ftCo_PassiveWall.c and ftCo_0C35.c.
Replaced manual collision-copy loop with a struct copy: CollData coll = *getFtColl(fp).
Removed #pragma force_active and forced literal/data declarations from ftCo_0C35.c once no longer needed for the matched object.
Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(4), and PAD_STACK(16) in places where dummy locals had been used to shape the stack.
Adjusted inline/static behavior by changing inlineA0 in ftCo_0C35.c from static inline to static void.
Used explicit float/f64 casts and locals to steer codegen, including fp->facing_dir > (f64) 0.0F and a local drift variable before ftCommon_8007D440.
Simplified goto exit control flow in ftCo_AirCatch_Anim while preserving the final ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining check.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether only the data section was not matching; suggested unlinking/reverting to NonMatching if so, but said struct breakage needed to be fixed first.
review_feedback.lesson: Separate text-code matching issues from data-section matching issues, and do not paper over a bad struct layout if it affects other code.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching CollData size or fields after ecb_lock was moved out.
Check whether ItemLink::unk after x30_collData was later named or refined.
Search for remaining coll_data.ecb_lock references to confirm no stale accesses survived.
Look for future PRs fully matching ftCo_AirCatch.c after the CollData copy improvement.
Audit other weird copy loops around embedded structs for similar off-by-one-field layout mistakes.
